results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE OVERARCHING GOAL OF THIS RESEARCH IS TO DEVELOP AN ALTERNATIVE UPGRADING TECHNIQUE CAPABLE OF INTEGRATING (I.E., REDUCING THE NUMBER OF TREATMENT UNITS) AND INTENSIFYING (I.E., DECREASING POWER CONSUMPTION) THE PURIFICATION PROCESS. THE TEAM WILL CONTINUE TO IMPROVE THE ADSORPTION PERFORMANCE AND LONG-TERM STABILITY OF THE FINAL AMINOSILICA CANDIDATE.ACTIVITIES:THE PROJECT SEEKS TO IDENTIFY THE OPTIMAL AMINOSILICA CANDIDATE AND EVALUATE THEIR ABILITY TO EFFECTIVELY AND SIMULTANEOUSLY REMOVE TARGET IMPURITIES, SUCH AS CARBON DIOXIDE, WATER VAPOR, AND HYDROGEN SULFIDE. THIS WILL BE ACCOMPLISHED IN THE FOLLOWING TASKS: 1) IMPROVE THE OXIDATION STABILITY OF AMINOSILICA; 2) STUDY THE REMOVAL OF OTHER BIOGAS AND LANDFILL GAS IMPURITIES; 3) ASSESS THE LONG-TERM STABILITY OF AMINOSILICA DURING CYCLING. THE ULTIMATE GOAL IS TO INTEGRATE THE BIOGAS AND LANDFILL GAS PURIFICATION PROCESS INTO ONE SINGLE STAGE.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES WILL INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL REPORTS DOCUMENTING THE PROGRESS OF THEIR EFFORTS TO OPTIMIZE THE MADE-TO-ORDER ADSORBENT MATERIALS THAT CAN SIMULTANEOUSLY AND EFFECTIVELY REMOVE TARGET IMPURITIES FROM BIOGAS AND LANDFILL GAS TO PRODUCE HIGH-PURITY BIOMETHANE.  DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROGRAM WILL INCLUDE FARM OWNERS AND LANDFILL OPERATORS MANAGE HAZARDOUS GASES, STATE AND LOCAL GOVERNMENTS AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC.
